chamberlain.def: 室內侍從、管家、宮廷大臣的人物 (單數)
chamberlain.ex: The Lord Chamberlain is a very senior official of the royal court.

chambermaid.ex: The efficient chambermaid cleaned the hotel guest room daily.

chameleon.def: 變色龍、反覆無常的人的人物 (單數)
chameleon.ex: A changing chameleon blended perfectly into the green leaves.

chamfer.def: 倒角、圓頭、倒角的人的人、倒角地 (倒)
chamfer.ex: He used a special tool to cut a chamfer on the wood edge.

chamois/shammy.ex: He used a soft chamois leather to clean the car windows.

chamomile/camomile.ex: A cup of hot chamomile tea helps her to sleep better tonight.

champ.def: (口)冠軍、咀嚼聲、大口吃的人的人物、吃聲地 (冠)
champ.ex: The world champ defended his titles with great skill today.

champagne.ex: They toasted the happy couple with a glass of champagne.

champion.def: 冠軍、捍衛者、優秀的、捍衛、擁護的人的人物、捍衛地 (冠)
champion.col: champion athlete, champion of human rights
champion.ex: She is a famous champion of human rights and social justice.

championship.def: 錦標賽、捍衛、冠軍地位的人物 (單數)
championship.ex: He is planning to enter the world golf championship next month.

chance.def: 機會、偶然、可能性、冒險、碰巧、機會的人的人物、偶然地 (機)
chance.col: take a chance, by chance, fighting chance, games of chance
chance.ex: I met my old school friend completely by chance in town.

chancel.ex: The rebuilt chancel of the church dates back to the 14th century.

chancellery.def: 首相官邸、總理公署的人物 (單數)
chancellery.ex: The foreign minister was summoned to the state chancellery today.

chancellor.def: 大臣、總理、校長、法官的人物 (單數)
chancellor.ex: The German chancellor gave a long speech about the economy.

chancellorship.def: 校長(或總理等)職位的人物 (單數)
chancellorship.ex: Economic reform was the main focus during his chancellorship.

chancery.def: 大法官法院、公文書館的人物 (單數)
chancery.ex: The complex property dispute was settled in the court of chancery.

chancre.ex: A hard chancre is often the first visible sign of the disease.

chancy.def: 冒險的、不確定的、僥倖地 (冒)
chancy.ex: Investing in new startups can be a very chancy business.

chandelier.ex: A massive crystal chandelier hung from the ballroom ceiling.

chandler.def: 雜貨商、蠟燭商人物 (單數)
chandler.ex: The ship chandler supplied the crew with all necessary food.

change.def: 改變、零錢、找零、變更的人的人物、變更地 (變)
change.col: climate change, change of heart, spare change, small change
change.ex: The leaves begin to change color in the early autumn months.

changeability.ex: The extreme changeability of mountain weather is well known.

changeable.def: 易變的、不穩定的人的人物
changeable.ex: The mountain weather is very changeable, so take a coat.

changed.def: 改變了的的人的人 (或物) (變)
changed.ex: He returned from his long travels a completely changed man.

changeful.ex: Her changeful mood made it difficult to work with her today.

changeless.def: 不變的、始終如一地 (不變)
changeless.ex: They promised each other their deep and changeless love.

changeling.def: 被偷換的孩子、低能兒、易變的人人物 (單數)
changeling.ex: Local legends tell stories of fairies stealing a human changeling.

changeover.def: 轉變、轉換、更換的人 (或物) (換)
changeover.ex: The system changeover will take place during the long weekend.

changer.def: 改變者、轉換器的人物 (單數)
changer.ex: The new battery technology could be a real game changer.

changing.def: 變化的、正在改變的人 (或物) (變)
changing.ex: Please leave your bag in the changing room before the game.

channel.def: 海峽、頻道、途徑、管道、引導的人物、引導地 (道)
channel.col: english channel, television channel, through official channels
channel.ex: The swimmers managed to cross the cold English Channel today.

channelize/channelise.def: 引導、開闢水道的人的人、引導地 (導)
channelize/channelise.ex: We need to channelize our efforts into the key priorities.

chant.def: 聖歌、反覆吟唱、叫喊、吟頌的人的人物、吟唱地 (吟)
chant.ex: The crowd began a rhythmic chant to support their local team.

chanterelle.ex: We found some delicious yellow chanterelles in the forest.

chantey/shanty.ex: The sailors sang a lively sea chantey as they worked hard.

chanticleer.def: 公雞 (文學用語)人物 (單數)
chanticleer.ex: The proud chanticleer crowed loudly at the dawn of the day.

chanting.def: 吟唱、反覆叫喊的人 (或物) (吟)
chanting.ex: The slow chanting of the monks filled the quiet monastery.

chaos.def: 混亂、無秩序的人物 (單數)
chaos.ex: The sudden power cut threw the entire city into total chaos.

chaotic.def: 混亂的、無秩序的人的人物
chaotic.ex: The crowded market was a loud and very chaotic situation.

chaotically.ex: The traffic moved chaotically through the busy city streets.
